informative.title: Pan-Tompkins-style ECG R-peak detection
informative.summary: Detects ordered R-wave occurrence times from a sampled ECG signal.
informative.description: This Pan-Tompkins-style detector is implemented in Biosigmat using bandpass filtering, derivative filtering, squaring, moving-window integration, peak detection, and peak refinement. The current implementation follows the Pan-Tompkins processing style but is not a byte-for-byte reproduction of the original paper.
notes: Only the primary R-peak timing output is normative in this draft.
Biosigmat's additional intermediate MATLAB outputs are implementation-specific.
Insufficient-data behavior remains unspecified in this draft.

references.note: Algorithm origin for the Pan-Tompkins-style processing chain.

definitions.text: Apply bandpass filtering, derivative filtering, squaring, moving-window integration, peak detection, and peak refinement to the ECG signal.
Detected R-wave occurrence times in seconds, sorted in ascending order.

behavior.nan_handling: NaN samples in the ECG signal are preserved through filtering as the implementation permits. Detections should not be produced inside NaN-corrupted regions in a future shared NaN case; the case in this draft contains no NaN samples.
behavior.empty_input: Empty ECG input is invalid; the exact failure mechanism is implementation-specific.
behavior.input_orientation: Treat ECG input as a one-dimensional vector regardless of row or column orientation. R-peak times are conceptually a one-dimensional ordered vector.
